hero_subtitle: SFTY streamlines digital asset compliance by connecting multiple KYC providers into one operational layer that manages onboarding, case routing, and audit-ready evidence seamlessly.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Onboarding slows when KYC providers disconnect
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to unify data and actions across multiple KYC providers, creating manual handoffs and inconsistent onboarding workflows. This fragmentation leads to slower reviews, weak audit trails, and difficulty maintaining a clear compliance record for each customer.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY aggregates outputs from diverse KYC providers into one unified onboarding workflow that routes reviews, preserves audit evidence and maintains consistent, traceable records for faster compliance decisions.
workflow_title: From multiple KYC checks to approval
workflow_description: SFTY aggregates and normalizes outputs from multiple KYC providers then applies rules to route reviews, create cases, record decisions and preserve evidence.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One compliance layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects multiple KYC provider outputs into unified workflows, decisions, cases, and audit-ready evidence without replacing existing onboarding tools.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing multiple KYC providers by preserving audit-ready evidence and enabling enterprise deployment with controlled access and compliance governance.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY streamline multi-KYC provider workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how KYC results combine into centralized case workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence is preserved across KYC providers
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Compare deployment options for provider-neutral KYC orchestration
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for multi-KYC orchestration?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects outputs from multiple KYC providers into connected workflows, enabling digital asset firms to orchestrate onboarding, reviews, cases and decisions securely and transparently without fragmenting operations.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage multiple KYC providers?
faq_2_answer: SFTY normalizes and aggregates provider data, applies internal policies, routes cases based on risk or jurisdiction, and structures approvals for a consistent, scalable KYC onboarding process.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready KYC evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, provider outputs and case evidence in a structured format that supports audit readiness and traceability of onboarding decisions across multiple KYC providers.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing onboarding stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates with existing KYC vendors and onboarding platforms without requiring replacement, layering orchestration and case management on top of current compliance stacks.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC providers in workflows?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC providers; it aggregates their outputs into one operational layer to improve workflow efficiency and auditability.
final_title: Connect multiple KYC providers into one workflow
final_description: Review how SFTY combines KYC outputs into structured onboarding cases, decision logs and audit-ready evidence for VASPs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
